meta.report: Step 1 Track A: E0 Home-Run Dependence & Behavioral Fragility Audit

concentration.note: Top 5 trades contribute 90% of net PnL. Remaining 182 trades net to -$94K.

fat_tails.interpretation: Extreme positive skew and leptokurtosis. Returns are far from normal. A few massive wins drive the distribution.

giveback.interpretation: Most trades give back moderate MFE. Short holds give back the most. Winners that run long (14d+) keep most of their gain (mean giveback 0.33). Trail stop captures the move well for long-duration trades.

native_view.interpretation: Removing top 6 trades (3.1%) collapses CAGR below zero. Classic cliff-edge.

unit_size_view.interpretation: Unit-size view is slightly more resilient (zero-cross at 12 vs 6 trades) because compounding amplifies large wins.

cliff_edge.interpretation: Both views show cliff-edge behavior. The single largest trade (episode 163 in native, episode 57 in unit-size) causes disproportionate damage when removed. This is a structural feature of trend-following with high positive skew.

skip_after_n_losses.overall_judgment: HARMFUL at N<=3, NEUTRAL at N>=4
skip_after_n_losses.interpretation: Skipping after 2-3 consecutive losses is destructive because it catches top-10 winning trades that follow loss clusters. The strategy's big winners are unpredictable and can follow drawdown periods. Behavioral skipping destroys the home-run tail.

style_classification.rationale: Top 5 trades = 90% of net PnL. Top 10 trades = 143% (remaining trades net negative). CAGR collapses to zero after removing 6 of 192 trades (3.1%). Gini = 0.61, skew = 3.66, effective N = 57 of 192. Classic positively-skewed trend-following payoff profile.
style_classification.cliff_vs_smooth_rationale: Cliff score 3.60 (terminal) and 17.36 (CAGR) in native view both exceed the 3.0 threshold. The CAGR collapse from 19.3% to -26.1% when removing trade #6 (a single $30K trade) is the defining cliff. Dependence is concentrated, not gradual.

answers_to_spec_questions.q1_would_e0_be_profitable_without_top_5_: Barely. With top 5 removed (native), terminal value = $31,435 (from $228,480), CAGR drops from 61.8% to 19.3%, Sharpe from 1.14 to 0.77. Strategy survives but at a fraction of its value. Remove one more (#6) and it collapses below zero.
answers_to_spec_questions.q2_is_dependence_cliff_like_or_smooth: Cliff-like. The CAGR sensitivity curve shows an abrupt collapse at trade #6 (cliff score 17.36). The terminal curve has a cliff at trade #1 (score 3.60). Both views confirm single-point collapse rather than gradual degradation.
answers_to_spec_questions.q3_does_skipping_after_N_losses_hurt: Yes for N<=3, No for N>=4. At N=2, skipping 37 trades destroys 23% of Sharpe and catches 3 top-10 winners. At N=4-5, the skip count drops to 5-8 trades with negligible Sharpe impact. The big winners follow loss clusters.
answers_to_spec_questions.q4_how_much_mfe_is_given_back: Median giveback ratio 1.21x, meaning the typical trade exits with slightly less than half its peak gain. Short-duration trades (<1d) give back 17x MFE on average (mostly losers that barely moved). Long-duration winners (14d+) give back only 0.33x — the trail stop works well for sustained moves.
answers_to_spec_questions.q5_overall_home_run_dependence_grade: HIGH. E0 is unambiguously a home-run dependent strategy. 3.1% of trades (6 of 192) determine whether the strategy is profitable at all. The payoff profile has extreme positive skew (3.66), the Gini (0.61) indicates high concentration, and the cliff-edge is detected in both native and unit-size views. This is a structural property of trend-following, not a deficiency — but it means every signal must be taken.
